design_philosophy.core_loop: Gather resources (ore/gems via miners) → construct buildings and units → destroy enemy base while defending own base → achieve capitulation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Economic competition and unit composition balance; players must defend income sources (refineries, derricks) while mounting offensive pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a Cold War superpower faction with distinct military doctrine, leveraging faction-specific strengths (Soviet heavy firepower vs. Allied maneuverability) to achieve dominance
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Asymmetric faction design where Soviet and Allied armies have fundamentally different strategic pacing—Soviets dominate early rushes with cheaper, faster infantry and heavier tanks; Allies excel late-game with advanced naval units. Playing a specific nation materially changes strategy, not just aesthetics. First C&C RTS without mission select screens, forcing commitment to level conditions.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Skirmish mode allows customization of battle rules and settings. Single-player campaigns present fixed AI difficulty; no documented difficulty selector for campaigns

progression.upgrade_system: Unit and building construction via tech tree; faction-specific unique units and abilities unlock through base development
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through 12-mission campaigns (Allied or Soviet), with Boot Camp tutorial leading chronologically into Allied campaign. Each mission has specific objectives (destroy structure, capture building, defend position) that vary mission-to-mission
replayability_hooks: Two full 12-mission campaigns with distinct stories and units (Allied vs. Soviet)
Skirmish mode with customizable battle parameters
Faction asymmetry encourages replaying campaign with opposite side for different strategic experience
Yuri's Revenge expansion adds third playable faction and campaign

world_building_techniques: Full-motion video cutscenes between missions and during gameplay
Ensemble cast (Ray Wise, Udo Kier, Kari Wuhrer, Barry Corbin) delivering story
Alternate-ending campaign structure where player choice of faction (Soviet vs. Allied) determines narrative path
Cold War setting with loose real-life historical faction inspiration

faction_indicators: Soviet faction: emphasis on heavy armor, firepower, early-game dominance, expensive but durable units (Heavy Rhino Tank, V3 Rocket Launchers)
Allied faction: emphasis on maneuverability, cost-efficiency, late-game advanced capabilities, naval warfare superiority
Conscript (Soviet) vs. G.I. (Allied) infantry contrast exemplifies faction identity
danger_signals: Early Soviet rush with cheap, fast-trained Conscripts poses immediate threat
Allied late-game unit composition with naval units signals technological advantage
Control of neutral oil derricks indicates permanent income advantage
environmental_storytelling: Neutral ore and gem deposits on maps drive strategic positioning of refineries
Neutral oil derricks as contested resources create territorial conflict
Player base construction and defensive structures reflect chosen faction aesthetic
